_metadata.description: Decision policy layer for PolyJarvis. Encodes the EVALUATION FRAMEWORK for each scientific decision the agent makes, rather than prescribing the action. The Planner consults this when proposing a run_plan.json; the Critic ENFORCES that every decision in the plan addresses each criterion in its policy and cites evidence. Modeled on the electrostatics_decision_guide pattern in polymer_rules.json. 'The agent is free, but the evaluation framework is fixed.'
_metadata.see_also: polymer_rules.json:electrostatics_decision_guide is the prototype for this structure (D-03 policy).

evidence_levels.high: Class-specific MD validation paper reproduces a target property (density or Tg) within ~10%, with DOI.
evidence_levels.medium: Force field validated for a related class or by builder constraint; no class-specific MD Tg/density paper yet.
evidence_levels.low: No validation; choice made by analogy or builder availability only. Reasoned planning REQUIRED.

confidence_gate.description: Controls planning DEPTH. Read class 'confidence' from polymer_rules.json. This is the 'uncertainty-driven planning' mechanism — grounded in an existing field so reproducible fixed-seed runs are never disturbed.

high.behavior: Planner transcribes polymer_rules.json defaults verbatim into run_plan.json decided_params (a subset of class keys with identical values). Critic auto-approves (trivial pass, 0 reasoning rounds). Worker prompts are byte-identical to the pre-architecture pipeline. Use fixed seeds from guides/REVISION_PARAMS.md.
high.rationale: These classes are validated; reproducibility outranks re-derivation. Do not re-reason a settled, cited decision.

medium_low_or_offtable.behavior: Planner reasons each decision against its policy below, records evidence/confidence/alternatives, names the dominant uncertainty and an optional uncertainty-reduction probe. Critic challenges; loop until approved or escalate (max 2 critic rounds).
medium_low_or_offtable.applies_to: confidence in {medium, low} OR polymer_class absent from polymer_rules.json.classes

require: mpi>=4 for any PPPM/charged class2 system (mpi=1 starves kspace ~75x — never launch it)
UA classes (TraPPE: PHYC/PDIE) route to CPU engine (gpu_ids empty), not GPU mpi=1
small (<10k-atom) cells use 1 GPU per run, not 4 (comm overhead, GPU underfill)
Sum of mpi across concurrent runs <= 18 physical cores (one GPU-heavy run per GPU)
hardware.rationale: Hardware config is a deterministic function of FF cost structure (UA lj/cut vs PCFF class2+pppm) and cell size, so it belongs in policy, not ad-hoc submit-time choice. The two live anti-patterns (mpi=1 PCFF ~2.4 ns/day; UA on GPU mpi=1) are both ~7-75x slower than the policy default. gen_prompt.py derives mpi/engine from hardware_policy when the CLI omits them; specific gpu_ids stay runtime via scripts/pick_gpu.py.

require: the chosen FF must have parameter coverage for every atom type in the SMILES
force field assigned AFTER polymerization for RadonPy path (never before; cross-stage rule 0)
forcefield.rationale: FF choice dominates property accuracy. A choice without literature_support + validation_data is low-confidence and the Critic must flag it. Alternatives with their known error must be recorded (e.g. GAFF2_mod Tg error >45% for PMMA).

require: charge method consistent with the chosen FF (e.g. TraPPE-UA embeds charges; PCFF uses bond-increment/Gasteiger)
charges.rationale: Apolar UA backbones embed charges in the FF (no QM). Polar all-atom systems need explicit charges. Solubility/CED accuracy degrades for Gasteiger-charged apolar systems — flag charge_method downstream.

require: heteroatom-containing or |q|>0.1e backbones MUST use pppm; pure C/H backbones MAY use lj_cut
electrostatics.rationale: Already encoded as polymer_rules.json:electrostatics_decision_guide. This policy points the Critic at that guide; neglecting long-range Coulomb biases density/Tg by 5-20% for polar polymers.

require: DP above the Fox-Flory plateau (DP>=20) for Tg targets
bulk_modulus targets need DP/nchain above the entanglement-MW threshold (per-class)
cell large enough to avoid self-imaging at the chosen cutoff_A
system_size.rationale: Too-small DP biases Tg low and K high. Too-large wastes GPU budget. The probe option (fast small-cell density screen, ROADMAP E3) reduces finite-size uncertainty cheaply before committing the full cell.

require: check_equilibration_comprehensive overall_pass=True before ANY property extraction (cross-stage rule 2); EXCEPT glassy DP>=30 systems, which use require_glassy (see below)
require_glassy: for regime=glassy AND DP>=30: gate on density plateau within experimental range AND density homogeneity CV<0.25 AND P2 nematic<0.10 AND energy drift/SEM within bounds. The melt chain-self-diffusion metrics (C(t) decay, MSD diffusivity, MSID slope, Rg chain-chain CV) are ADVISORY (logged, non-blocking). overall_pass may be False on these advisory metrics without blocking property extraction.
equilibration.rationale: This is the philosophy's 'convince me it is equilibrated' gate — already implemented as check_equilibration_comprehensive. The policy makes it a hard plan success_criterion, not an afterthought. The Validator enforces it after the equil stage.
equilibration.rationale_glassy: For glassy high-MW polymers (DP>=30) melt chain self-diffusion is unattainable on MD timescales (measured tau_relax ~3.6e9 ps for DP=60 PVC; a 1-2 ns extend moves C(t) only ~0.4%->0.5%), so overall_pass=True is unsatisfiable by construction. The 5-cycle high-T anneal substitutes for melt self-diffusion. The metrics that actually bound structural quality for density/Tg/glassy-K_T — density plateau, homogeneity CV, P2 — are gating; the diffusion-limited metrics are advisory. Authorized 2026-06-20 (PVC1 route-back, user-approved). Applies campaign-wide to glassy revision polymers.

require: never report Tg without verifying bilinear fit R^2 (cross-stage rule 3)
T sweep range must bracket experimental_tg_K with margin
tg_protocol.rationale: A too-narrow T range gives R^2<0.80 / <4 bins (known /recover failure mode). Planning the range from experimental_tg_K +/- margin catches this before the sweep runs.

require: glassy bulk_modulus via Born+NVT (rate-free, full elastic tensor); rubbery via Murnaghan EOS sweep; volume fluctuation is cross-check only; deform is fallback only
is_glassy from measured Tg>300K when tg requested, else from experimental_tg_K glassy_hint
property_method.rationale: Method must match phase. Born is glassy-only/CPU-only and cannot be the unified path; Murnaghan is the GPU-compatible both-phase candidate (ROADMAP I2). Deform overestimates K 20-50% and is a tertiary fallback.

stage_schema_requirements.description: Schema that every planned_stages entry must satisfy. The Critic enforces this in step 3 for reasoned plans; make_deterministic_plan.py satisfies it automatically via the STAGE_TRACK dict.

uncertainty_reduction_probes.description: Optional cheap experiments a reasoned plan may schedule BEFORE the full pipeline to reduce the dominant uncertainty. Each maps to an existing or planned ROADMAP capability.

fast_density_screen.mechanism: small-cell short NPT; compare density to experimental within +/-10%; max 2 FF retries (ROADMAP E3, currently BLOCKED on A1/A2 — record as planned, not executed, until unblocked).

literature_anchor.mechanism: search_ff_literature(smiles, name) -> reference FF + density/Tg targets + DOI (ROADMAP E2).

born_vs_murnaghan_crosscheck.reduces: bulk_modulus method validity for glassy polymers
born_vs_murnaghan_crosscheck.mechanism: run both on one glassy test polymer; agreement within ~10% promotes Murnaghan as glassy fallback (ROADMAP I2-A).

hardware_benchmark.reduces: mpi/gpu config uncertainty for an off-table FF or an unusually sized cell
hardware_benchmark.mechanism: scripts/benchmark_hardware.py sweeps the mpi/gpu matrix on the built .data per template cost class, reports ns/day + Pair/Kspace/Neigh/Comm breakdown, and names the winner; refines hardware_policy and TEMPLATE_DEFAULTS use_gpu. Runs politely around live runs (idle GPU + spare cores only).
